steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills the object of the preposition “by” in “The emulsifiers used by ______”. The chemists are the agents using the emulsifiers — no possession is expressed — so we need the plain plural noun, not a possessive form.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — cosmetic chemists',: “chemists'” is a plural possessive. The chemists own nothing in this sentence; they are simply the users. ✗
  • Choice B — cosmetic chemist's,: “chemist's” is a singular possessive, introducing both number error and an unneeded possessive. ✗
  • Choice C — cosmetic chemists,: Plain plural noun followed by a comma that correctly sets off the appositive “like palmitic acid.” No possessive is added because no possession exists. ✓
  • Choice D — cosmetic's chemists,: Applies the possessive to the wrong word; “cosmetic's” makes no sense here. ✗
C is correct.
